opportunity.title: Jeriko – an AI agent that runs directly inside your OS
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: ** OS-level agent integration introduces real problems around permission sandboxing and state persistence that wrapper-based agents avoid entirely. You'll find that true tool orchestration requires building your own memory layer—relying on external APIs breaks when the agent needs to remember context across sessions or compare tool outputs against cached results.
actions.reasoning: ** I have hands-on experience building AI agents, terminal tooling, and multi-provider LLM routing systems, including practical knowledge of cognitive architectures and agent loops.

opportunity.title: SysNav – An Intelligent Cockpit for DevOps (Local-First)
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: The "intelligent cockpit" concept highlights a crucial, often overlooked, area: cognitive load management for complex agent workflows. Routing LLMs based on task *and* cost is table stakes; the real difficulty lies in dynamically adjusting agent memory and tool selection based on observed performance – a feedback loop that’s far more challenging than initial architecture suggests. Expect significant overhead in maintaining a robust, self-diagnosing agent orchestration layer.
actions.reasoning: I've built systems routing LLM requests across providers and orchestrating tools for agents, so I have direct experience with the challenges SysNav addresses.
